title: Module 1
content:
Fundamentals of Tissue Engineering (T1)
Cell extracellular matrix interactions (Development and wound healing) -
Adhesion and migration - Proliferation - Differentiation – Apoptosis. Signal
transduction events during cell-extracellular matrix interactions - Integrin-
Mediated Signalling- Type I – type II – type III Interactions- Engineering
Tissue morphogenesis -   Relevance for tissue engineering

title: Module 2
content:
The Tissue Microenvironment: Cell Therapy and Bioreactor (T2)
Design
Principles
-
Cellular
Function
In
Vivo:
The
Tissue
Microenvironment and Communication – Cellularity – Dynamics -Size
and Geometry.
Importance of in vitro studies for engineering functional tissues - Influence
of selected in vitro culture parameters on the development and performance
of engineered tissues.  Bioreactors - Macrobioreactors – Comparison of
commercial Macrobioreactors – design principle. Microbioreactors -design
principles.

title: Module 3
content:
Advanced Biomaterials and scaffold fabrication Techniques (T3)
Advances in Antimicrobial and Osteoinductive Biomaterials – Current
challenges in biomaterials -  Antimicrobial Biomaterials - Osteoinductive
Biomaterials - Dual Functional Biomaterials.   Scaffold Fabrication
Techniques for Tissue Engineering Applications - Freeze-Drying -
Solvent Casting/Particle Leaching - Phase Separation- Electrospinning- Gas
Foaming

title: Module 4
content:
Biofabrication in Tissue Engineering (T3)
Biofabrication Strategies- Inkjet Bioprinting - Laser-assisted Bioprinting -
Extrusion Bioprinting - Melt Electrowriting (MEW). Criteria for
Biomaterial Design and Selection – Biocompatibility - Material Properties
- Structural Properties - Degradation. Commonly Used Biofabrication
Materials - Naturally Derived Biomaterials - Synthetic Biomaterials.
Future Perspectives of biofabrication technologies - Multi-scale
Biofabrication – Vascularization - Biomaterial Development.
